Summary: Authenticated user views account balance on dashboard
Description: Verify that an authenticated user can successfully view their account balance displayed on the dashboard
Action:
1. Navigate to the bank application login page
2. Enter valid credentials (username and password)
3. Click the Login button
4. Wait for dashboard to load
5. Observe the account balance section on the dashboard
Data Steps: Valid user credentials: username=testuser@bank.com, password=ValidPass123!, Account ID=ACC-12345, Expected balance=$1,234.56
Expected Results: User is authenticated successfully and redirected to dashboard. Account balance is displayed prominently showing the correct amount in USD format with 2 decimal places

Description: Verify that the account balance automatically updates with real-time data every 30 seconds without manual page refresh
Action:
1. Login with valid credentials
2. Navigate to dashboard
3. Note the current balance and timestamp
4. Make an external transaction to change balance
5. Wait exactly 30 seconds without refreshing
6. Observe if balance updates automatically
7. Verify network calls in browser developer tools
Data Steps: Valid user credentials, Initial balance=$5,000.00, External deposit amount=$100.00, Expected new balance=$5,100.00
Expected Results: Balance automatically refreshes at 30-second intervals. Network tab shows GET /api/accounts/{id}/balance call every 30 seconds. Updated balance reflects the new amount after refresh cycle

Summary: Unauthenticated user redirected to login page
Description: Verify that users without valid authentication are redirected to the login page when attempting to access the dashboard
Action:
1. Clear all browser cookies and session storage
2. Attempt to directly access dashboard URL (/dashboard)
3. Observe the page behavior
4. Verify the redirect URL
Data Steps: No authentication credentials, Direct URL access to dashboard: https://app.bank.com/dashboard
Expected Results: User is immediately redirected to the login page (/login). Dashboard content is not visible. No balance information is exposed. URL shows login page path

Summary: Balance displays in USD format with 2 decimal places
Description: Verify that the account balance is formatted correctly with USD currency symbol and exactly 2 decimal places
Action:
1. Login with valid credentials
2. Navigate to dashboard
3. Observe the balance display format
4. Verify currency symbol position
5. Verify decimal places count
6. Check for thousand separators if applicable
Data Steps: User with balance of 12345.6789 in database, Expected display format=$12,345.68
Expected Results: Balance displays with $ symbol prefix, exactly 2 decimal places (rounded if necessary), comma thousand separators for amounts over 999.99. Example: $12,345.68

Description: Verify that an account with zero balance displays as $0.00 and not as blank, empty, or null
Action:
1. Login with user account having zero balance
2. Navigate to dashboard
3. Observe the balance display area
4. Verify the exact text shown for balance
Data Steps: User credentials for zero-balance account: username=zerobalance@bank.com, password=ValidPass123!, Account balance=0
Expected Results: Balance section displays exactly $0.00. Field is not blank, empty, null, or showing any error message. Format follows USD standard with dollar sign and two decimal places

Description: Verify that the balance API endpoint responds within the required 2-second threshold under normal conditions
Action:
1. Login with valid credentials
2. Open browser developer tools Network tab
3. Navigate to dashboard or trigger balance refresh
4. Locate the GET /api/accounts/{id}/balance request
5. Record the response time
6. Repeat 5 times and calculate average
Data Steps: Valid authentication token, Account ID=ACC-12345, Performance threshold=2000ms
Expected Results: Each API call to /api/accounts/{id}/balance completes in under 2 seconds. Average response time across multiple calls remains below threshold. No timeout errors occur

Summary: Invalid Bearer token returns unauthorized error
Description: Verify that requests with invalid or malformed Bearer tokens are rejected with appropriate error response
Action:
1. Open API testing tool (Postman/curl)
2. Send GET request to /api/accounts/{id}/balance
3. Include invalid Bearer token in Authorization header
4. Observe response status and body
5. Repeat with malformed token format
Data Steps: Invalid token=Bearer invalid_token_12345, Malformed token=Bearer xyz!@#$%^&*, Expired token=Bearer expired_token_abc, Account ID=ACC-12345
Expected Results: API returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized status. Response body contains appropriate error message. No balance data is leaked in error response. Request is logged for security audit

Description: Verify that API requests without Authorization header are properly rejected
Action:
1. Open API testing tool
2. Send GET request to /api/accounts/{id}/balance
3. Do not include any Authorization header
4. Observe response status and body
Data Steps: Account ID=ACC-12345, No Authorization header present
Expected Results: API returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized status. Error message indicates missing authentication. No balance information is returned. System does not crash or expose sensitive data

Summary: Access balance of another user's account
Description: Verify that authenticated users cannot access balance information of other users' accounts (IDOR prevention)
Action:
1. Login as User A with valid credentials
2. Capture the valid Bearer token
3. Send GET request to /api/accounts/{User_B_ID}/balance
4. Use User A's token but User B's account ID
5. Observe response
Data Steps: User A token=Bearer valid_token_userA, User B Account ID=ACC-67890 (belongs to different user)
Expected Results: API returns HTTP 403 Forbidden status. Error message indicates access denied. User B's balance is not exposed. Attempt is logged for security monitoring

Summary: SQL injection attempt in account ID parameter
Description: Verify that the API is protected against SQL injection attacks through the account ID parameter
Action:
1. Login with valid credentials
2. Capture valid Bearer token
3. Send GET request with SQL injection payload in account ID
4. Try multiple injection patterns
5. Observe responses for each attempt
Data Steps: Injection payloads: 1) ACC-12345' OR '1'='1, 2) ACC-12345; DROP TABLE accounts;--, 3) ACC-12345 UNION SELECT * FROM users--, Valid Bearer token
Expected Results: API returns HTTP 400 Bad Request or sanitizes input. No database errors exposed. No unauthorized data returned. Application continues to function normally. Attack attempts are logged

Description: Verify that very large balance amounts are displayed correctly with proper formatting
Action:
1. Login with user having large balance
2. Navigate to dashboard
3. Observe balance display
4. Verify all digits are visible
5. Check thousand separator placement
Data Steps: User with balance=$999,999,999.99 (near billion), User credentials for high-balance test account
Expected Results: Balance displays correctly as $999,999,999.99. All digits are visible without truncation. Thousand separators are correctly placed. No overflow or display issues occur

Description: Verify that negative balance (overdraft) is displayed correctly with appropriate formatting and visual indication
Action:
1. Login with user account in overdraft status
2. Navigate to dashboard
3. Observe the balance display
4. Check for negative indicator and formatting
Data Steps: User with negative balance=-$150.75 (overdraft account), Test account credentials
Expected Results: Negative balance displays with minus sign or parentheses: -$150.75 or ($150.75). Visual indication of negative status (red color or warning). Format maintains 2 decimal places

Description: Verify proper handling when user session expires during the 30-second auto-refresh cycle
Action:
1. Login with valid credentials
2. Navigate to dashboard
3. Wait for balance to display
4. Manually expire/invalidate the session token on server side
5. Wait for next 30-second refresh cycle
6. Observe application behavior
Data Steps: Valid user session initially, Session invalidation after initial load, Refresh cycle timing=30 seconds
Expected Results: Application detects authentication failure on refresh. User is notified of session expiry. User is redirected to login page. No sensitive error details exposed. Previous balance data is cleared from view

Summary: API server unavailable during balance fetch
Description: Verify graceful handling when the balance API endpoint is unavailable or returns server error
Action:
1. Login with valid credentials
2. Simulate API server downtime or 500 error
3. Navigate to dashboard or wait for refresh
4. Observe error handling
5. Verify retry mechanism if implemented
Data Steps: Valid user credentials, Simulated API response: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error or connection timeout
Expected Results: User-friendly error message is displayed (not technical stack trace). Application suggests retry or displays last known balance with timestamp. System does not crash. Error is logged for monitoring

Description: Verify correct rounding behavior when balance contains more than 2 decimal places from backend
Action:
1. Setup account with balance having multiple decimal places in database
2. Login with valid credentials
3. Navigate to dashboard
4. Observe displayed balance
5. Verify rounding logic (standard banker's rounding)
Data Steps: Database balance values: 1) 100.994 (should round to $100.99), 2) 100.995 (should round to $101.00), 3) 100.996 (should round to $101.00)
Expected Results: Balance is rounded to exactly 2 decimal places using standard rounding rules. 100.994 displays as $100.99. 100.995 displays as $101.00. No floating-point display errors occur
